lineup_source: Projected XI (4-2-3-1) under Ralf Rangnick, based on Austria's June 2026 friendly vs Tunisia and World Cup squad announcement

players.name: Alexander Schlager
players.note: Born in Salzburg; no documented foreign heritage, typical Austrian background.

players.name: Konrad Laimer
players.note: Born in Salzburg to Austrian parents; father involved in local football.

players.name: Stefan Posch
players.note: Born in Judenburg, Styria; no documented foreign heritage.

players.name: Philipp Lienhart
players.note: Born in Lilienfeld, Lower Austria; no documented foreign heritage.

players.name: David Alaba
players.note: Born in Vienna to a Yoruba Nigerian father, George Alaba, and a Filipina mother, Gina Alaba, both immigrants to Austria.

players.name: Nicolas Seiwald
players.note: Born in Kuchl, Salzburg; no documented foreign heritage.

players.name: Xaver Schlager
players.note: Born in Linz, raised in St. Valentin; parents Uwe and Doris Schlager are local Austrians.

players.name: Romano Schmid
players.note: Born in Graz; despite the Italian-sounding first name, no documented Italian family heritage found.

players.name: Marcel Sabitzer
players.note: Born in Wels; son of Herfried Sabitzer, a former Austria international footballer.

players.name: Michael Gregoritsch
players.note: Born in Graz; family is of Slovenian origin via his father's side, with father Werner Gregoritsch a former Austria U21 coach.

players.name: Marko Arnautovic
players.note: Born in Vienna to a Serbian father and Austrian mother; an Orthodox Christian who identifies closely with his Serbian roots.
